prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f+ electrophilic susceptibility, f- nucleophilic susceptibility, f0, and the dual descriptor) for acrolein; headline value reported is the largest electrophilic Fukui index (f+).
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting (RI) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: single-point analysis on the user-supplied acrolein.xyz coordinates (no geometry optimisation at DFT).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet ground state).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend; version per engine build).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles converged; all integrity checks passed. The engine emitted three warnings (reproduced verbatim below). They note charge-conservation checks and header presence; all checks reported ok.
Live .out log: the run streamed to the standard chemkit live log (path announced at job launch in the session transcript; see that file for full SCF and Mulliken-charge details). Output JSON saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpxavmi856/live.json and plot to /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/argo_o3/20260702-191815_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.

Headline result
• Most electrophilic atom: C4 (index 3) with f⁺ = 0.64232102 (dimensionless)
• Most nucleophilic atom: O1 (index 0) with f⁻ = 0.39388616 (dimensionless)

Warnings (verbatim from engine):
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
3. common header present

Integrity assessment: integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true (no convergence or consistency issues detected beyond the informational charge-conservation checks).
